title: Orthosiphon spicatus
taxonomy: Orthosiphon spicatus is classified in the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. This plant belongs to the genus Orthosiphon, which is a group of flowering plants. The species Orthosiphon spicatus is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The classification of Orthosiphon spicatus is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other species in the Lamiaceae family.
uses: Orthosiphon spicatus, also known as Java tea, is traditionally used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. It is said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing fever and relieving pain. In some cultures, Java tea is consumed to help manage urinary tract issues and support kidney health. Additionally, it is sometimes used to treat digestive problems and may have antimicrobial effects. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Orthosiphon spicatus for medicinal purposes.

intro: Orthosiphon spicatus, also known as cat's whiskers or Java tea, is a perennial plant native to Australia and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Lamiaceae. Traditionally, Orthosiphon spicatus is used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including urinary tract issues, kidney stones, and hypertension, due to its diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Orthosiphon spicatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Orthosiphon spicatus is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, as well as being used in capsule or tablet form to harness its health benefits.
